items.title: Choosing an encoding strategy
items.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  cat["categorical column"] --> kind{"order meaningful?"}
  kind -->|"no (nominal)"| card{"cardinality high?"}
  kind -->|"yes (ordinal)"| ord["ordinal map 0..K-1"]
  card -->|"low/medium"| ohe["one-hot columns"]
  card -->|"high"| tgt["target encode with CV + smoothing"]
  ohe --> model["model input"]
  ord --> model
  tgt --> model
items.caption: Nominal features use one-hot at modest cardinality or leakage-safe target encoding when levels explode; ordinal features use ordered integers.

items.title: Target encoding leakage vs safe out-of-fold
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  bad["global mean per category on full train"] --> leak["row sees its own y"]
  leak --> inflated["inflated offline metrics"]
  good["K-fold means on train only"] --> safe["each row encoded without own label"]
  safe --> honest["honest validation scores"]
items.caption: Computing category target means on all rows including the current one leaks labels into features; cross-fitted statistics avoid that.
